10.reason: [Loop condition and increment logic may skip characters or cause out-of-bounds access; also, variable-length loop with strlen() recomputed each time is inefficient but not a compilation error]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition and manual increment logic cause failure to print all characters, especially the last character(s). The loop does not handle characters after the last processed index.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop condition and C99 declaration may cause compilation error in C89; also logic error: never processes the last character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Declaring loop variable 'i' inside the for statement is not supported in C89/C90, causing a compilation error on strict/older compilers.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Logical error: the loop condition i<=strlen(s)-2 prevents the last character of the string from being processed and printed]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop condition and increment logic are flawed: it skips the last character and doesn't handle overlapping or consecutive '-' correctly. Also, the loop starts at index 1 and prints s[i-1] only when s[i] is not '-', which leads to missing the last character in many cases.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition and increment logic may skip characters or cause out-of-bounds access; also, variable-length loop with strlen() recomputed each time is inefficient but not a compilation error]

27.reason: Declaring loop variable 'j' inside for causes C89 compilation error; logically, starting j from 0 reprints s[i-1], causing duplicate output.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect: loop includes both endpoints, causing duplication in consecutive expansions. Should exclude endpoints because the first character is printed earlier and the last will be printed by next iteration.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Expansion loop includes the start character (j=0); this may cause double printing or incorrect output, and combined with the loop increment logic leads to missing characters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Declaring loop variable 'j' inside for causes C89 compilation error; logically, starting j from 0 reprints s[i-1], causing duplicate output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This loop includes both endpoints (j=0 to difference inclusive), causing duplication of the starting character which was already printed or will be printed again; expansion should start from s[i-1]+1 to s[i+1]-1.]

32.reason: [For the non-expansion case, this skips the character after the dash, causing it to be omitted. For expansion case, if endpoints not duplicated, it also skips the last character. The increment should be +1 to continue to next character, not +2.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [For the non-expansion case, this skips the character after the dash, causing it to be omitted. For expansion case, if endpoints not duplicated, it also skips the last character. The increment should be +1 to continue to next character, not +2.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Incrementing by 2 after a dash, together with the loop bound, causes the last character to be skipped.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Incrementing by 2 after handling a '-' assumes fixed 3-character pattern, but fails when multiple '-' are adjacent or at boundaries.]
